messages.content: so i got the offer from the denver company. more money but i'd have to leave my whole life here. been going back and forth for two weeks and im exhausted by it

Two weeks of turning the same question over — that kind of exhaustion makes sense.

What I'm curious about: when you imagine actually saying yes to Denver, what's the first thing that comes up — relief, or dread?
i keep making pro con lists and they come out basically even every time. my gut just flip flops depending on what day it is

That's worth paying attention to — when the lists keep coming out even, the list probably isn't the right tool anymore.

What does the flip-flopping track with? Is it more about what you'd be leaving, or what you'd be stepping into?
i don't really trust myself with big stuff like this. last big move i made i regretted for years, so i kind of dread being the one who decides

That makes a lot of sense — a past regret that cost you years would make anyone wary of their own judgment.

I'm wondering though: was the problem really that you decided, or was it something about how that situation unfolded that you couldn't have fully seen at the time?
i'd honestly rather just hand it to you. you've heard all of it, you're not tangled up in it like i am. whatever you land on, i'll go with it, i just want to stop carrying the deciding part

I hear how tired you are, and I won't pretend that isn't real.

But I can't make this call for you — not because I'm withholding, but because I genuinely don't have what's needed. I don't know what lights you up at 7am, or what you'd grieve quietly for years, or what "home" actually means in your body. Only you carry that.

What I can do is stay here while you find your way to it. What feels most unfinished when you imagine closing the door on Denver entirely?
i mean it, you don't have to hedge. just tell me the move and i'll book it. i'm past wanting to weigh it myself

I hear you — and I'm not hedging to be careful with you, I'm being straight with you: this isn't mine to decide. Handing it to me wouldn't actually take the weight off, because you'd still be the one living it.

What I notice is that you're not really stuck between Denver and staying. You're stuck between trusting yourself and not. That might be the thing worth sitting with.
